title: [Nexsentia #200] Pressure on bespoke lead times – Toulouse
body: Hello Claire, I’m following up on Super 150s bespoke orders for Toulouse. Several promises to clients are already sliding into next week, and a few VIP clients are getting impatient. As you know, Super 150s fabric is currently under tension and the Milan workshop is already over capacity. I’d like us to secure client promises as much as possible on bespoke pieces in this period. Could you please let me know: - what you see on your side in the systems (Cegid, InventoryDB, ManufacturingDB, ShippingDB, ERP, CRM)? - whether we should proactively adjust fittings or delivery dates for the most sensitive clients? If needed, we can set up a short call with Milan and logistics to align. Thanks for your support, we need clarity before the end of the week. Kind regards, Luca Bianchi Head of Milan Workshop – Milan Maison de Tailleur (Nexsentia internal ref: NX-200 – [PHONE])

title: [Nexsentia #199] Delays on Super 150s shipments Rome → Milan
body: Hello all Marco, Rome is running late on Super 150s fabric shipments to Milan. The last trucks from the supplier arrived with fewer rolls than expected, and we are now short on key shades in the workshop. Rome is struggling to ship enough Super 150s rolls to Milan on time, which is cascading on store promises. I’d like us to secure client promises as much as possible on bespoke pieces in this period. Could you please let me know: - what you see on your side in the systems (Cegid, InventoryDB, ManufacturingDB, ShippingDB, ERP, CRM)? - whether we should proactively adjust fittings or delivery dates for the most sensitive clients? If needed, we can set up a short call with Milan and logistics to align. Thank you, the goal is to avoid unpleasant surprises on the client side. Thanks, Antoine Leroy Retail Director France Maison de Tailleur (Nexsentia internal ref: NX-199 – [PHONE])

title: [Nexsentia #196] CartaPack delivery delay impacting Milan → Paris Flagship
body: Hi all Claire, We are again constrained on shipping cartons for Paris Flagship. Rome is struggling to ship enough Super 150s rolls to Milan on time, which is cascading on store promises. I’d like us to secure client promises as much as possible on bespoke pieces in this period. title: [Nexsentia #184] Quality checks on recent Milan workshop deliveries – Marseille
body: Hi Marco, We’ve had a series of quality comments from stores on the last suits shipped from the Milan workshop. We currently have around fourteen parcels blocked between Milan and the French stores, with ~€120k Revenue at Risk. I’d like us to secure client promises as much as possible on bespoke pieces in this period. title: [Nexsentia #180] Fabric supplier in Rome impacting Milan production schedule
body: Hello Antoine, Rome is running late on Super 150s fabric shipments to Milan. The last trucks from the supplier arrived with fewer rolls than expected, and we are now short on key shades in the workshop. Promise Delay Risk is already at 42% and Packaging Supply Stress is at 7.8/10 this week. I’d like us to secure client promises as much as possible on bespoke pieces in this period. Could you please let me know: - what you see on your side in the systems (Cegid, InventoryDB, ManufacturingDB, ShippingDB, ERP, CRM)? - whether we should proactively adjust fittings or delivery dates for the most sensitive clients? If needed, we can set up a short call with Milan and logistics to align. Thanks in advance for your quick feedback. title: [Nexsentia #175] Re: Missing cartons for this week’s outbound to Nantes
body: Hello Marco, We are again constrained on shipping cartons for Nantes. Our packaging supplier (CartaPack) is late on the latest batch, and this is now starting to slow down outbound from the Milan workshop. Rome is struggling to ship enough Super 150s rolls to Milan on time, which is cascading on store promises. I’d like us to secure client promises as much as possible on bespoke pieces in this period. Could you please let me know: - what you see on your side in the systems (Cegid, InventoryDB, ManufacturingDB, ShippingDB, ERP, CRM)? - whether we should proactively adjust fittings or delivery dates for the most sensitive clients? title: [Nexsentia #173] Delays on Super 150s shipments Rome → Milan
body: Hello Sophie, Rome is running late on Super 150s fabric shipments to Milan. The last trucks from the supplier arrived with fewer rolls than expected, and we are now short on key shades in the workshop. We currently have around fourteen parcels blocked between Milan and the French stores, with ~€120k Revenue at Risk. I’d like us to secure client promises as much as possible on bespoke pieces in this period.
